model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With a fixed budget of 256 optimizer steps, increasing batch size is a simple way to expose the model to much more of CIFAR-10 within the same step budget. This usually helps early-training validation loss on this kind of compact CNN more than small architecture tweaks, and it keeps the script simple and low-risk.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The larger batch already improved early-budget learning. The next low-complexity follow-up is to raise the Adam learning rate to better match that batch size and accelerate progress within only 256 steps.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The best gains so far came from letting the existing optimizer learn faster over more data. A small width increase is the next simplest capacity change to test, and this compact CNN is still small enough that a moderate bump in channels should remain safe under the fixed budget.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The budget-matched cosine schedule underperformed noticeably. Since the wider model improved slightly on top of that weaker setting, the clean next check is to keep the added capacity but disable scheduling and let Adam run at the stronger constant rate again.","secondary_modes":["layout","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The current recipe is strong and simple, so the next worthwhile change is a minimal optimizer-family swap. AdamW often works better than Adam once the model has enough capacity and a nontrivial weight decay term, and this tests that hypothesis without adding architectural complexity.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The move to AdamW helped slightly, which suggests the current setup still benefits from faster optimization. A moderate learning-rate increase is the next simplest way to push more progress into the fixed 256-step budget without changing the model or training loop.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"This workload is fixed by optimizer steps rather than epochs, and the earlier batch-size increase was the largest improvement so far. Pushing batch size higher is the simplest way to expose the model to even more training examples within the same 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The jump to batch size 512 was by far the strongest improvement, and the current AdamW rate was tuned on smaller batches. A modest additional LR increase is the cleanest follow-up to better match the new batch regime while preserving the rest of the recipe.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"This run is still dominated by early optimization under a fixed step budget, so heavy regularization is less likely to help than reaching a better fit quickly. A small reduction in AdamW weight decay is a low-risk way to preserve the strong large-batch recipe while easing underfitting pressure.","secondary_modes":["indexing","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The optimizer and large-batch recipe now look strong, so the next simple place to add capacity is the classifier bottleneck. The flattened feature map is relatively large, and a modest increase in hidden width may recover information currently being compressed too aggressively.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The classifier-width increase produced a meaningful gain, which suggests the head was still a bottleneck. A further moderate increase is a straightforward continuation of that successful direction with little added training complexity compared with changing the convolutional stack.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 768","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The dominant gains in this run have come from increasing data processed per optimizer step. Since the classifier-width bump appears to have saturated, the next highest-upside move is to push batch size further and exploit the fixed 256-step budget more aggressively.","secondary_modes":["topk","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 768","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Both recent upward moves regressed: the larger classifier head was slightly worse, and pushing batch size past 512 was clearly worse and much slower. The best next step is to move back to the strongest verified regime so subsequent experiments start from a better local optimum.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"This is now a short-horizon, large-batch AdamW regime. Lowering beta2 slightly is a targeted way to make second-moment estimates adapt faster within only 256 steps, which can improve optimization speed without changing the overall recipe.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The lower beta2 regressed, so the best next move is to restore the stronger AdamW moments and test a smaller learning-rate increase around the current optimum. This keeps the successful large-batch recipe intact while exploring whether the best point is slightly above 2e-3.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current optimizer and batch settings look strong, and the classifier bottleneck has already been relaxed. The next plausible source of headroom is a modest increase in convolutional width, which may improve feature quality without changing the training loop or overall architecture pattern.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The wider backbone regressed badly, so the safer path is to restore the stronger feature width and use the remaining budget on a lighter regularization test. In this short-horizon, large-batch setup, fully removing weight decay is a plausible way to squeeze out a bit more fit without reintroducing expensive architectural changes.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Removing weight decay regressed, so the stronger baseline is still the lightly regularized AdamW setup. With only a small remaining search horizon, the highest-value test is a narrow learning-rate sweep just above the current best point while restoring the better weight decay.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.4e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The most recent step improved validation loss again, so the clearest final move is to continue the successful local learning-rate sweep with one more small increase. This stays on the strongest trajectory discovered so far and preserves the rest of the verified high-performing recipe.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}
